goal: Build a reorient-and-deliver station: a 5cm cube starts ON ITS SIDE on a conveyor, hits a passive flip-wall at the conveyor end that tips it upright, lands in a small landing zone, then is picked by a Franka and placed upright in a destination bin. The function-gate uses require_upright=True so verification depends on the cube actually finishing upright (cube_up_vector · world_up >= 0.95).
thoughts:
Six REORIENT-01 specifics for the passive flip-wall design (extends CP-01: orientation rule, CPU dynamics, sleepThreshold, rubber-on-cube, target_source='curobo', cuRobo per-robot subscription tag — all inherited):
1) Cube starts with rotation [90, 0, 0] (90° around X) — long axis vertical → cube is laying on its side. Authored via xformOp:orient or set_attribute on rotation.
2) Flip-wall: a thin static collider at the conveyor's exit edge, height matching cube's top edge when on side (~0.05m above belt). When cube reaches wall, leading edge stops; top continues forward → cube tips around its bottom edge → lands upright.
3) Friction tuning: cube has rubber material (mu≈1.0). Belt has default-mu surface. Rubber-cube-on-default-belt gives ~0.75 effective friction. High enough for the belt to drive the cube forward, but not so high that cube can't slide off conveyor end.
4) Landing zone: a small rubber-tinted pad just past the flip-wall where the cube comes to rest upright. Robot picks from here.
5) Function-gate require_upright=True with default upright_tolerance_dot=0.95 — cube_up_vector · world_up >= 0.95 means within ~18° of vertical at sim end. The success threshold is intentionally tight to catch cubes that 'almost' flipped.
6) PHYSICS TUNING WARNING: passive flips depend on geometry + friction in a non-trivial way. This canonical is a STARTING POINT — flip-wall position, height, and cube initial orientation may need iteration. If cube doesn't reliably tip on first try, adjust flip-wall x position by 0.01-0.02m at a time, or its height up/down by 0.01m. Active flip-station (revolute joint) is a future alternative.
code:
# DomeLight + Ground (foundational)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Table
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.5, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# Franka at +X side (picks cube AFTER it has flipped upright)
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[1.0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# Conveyor — 2m wide so it spans cube start, flip-wall, AND landing zone.
# The whole reorient + delivery sequence happens on a single belt surface.
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/Conveyor",
    position=[0.0, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[2.0, 0.3, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

# Cube on its side: rotation_euler=[90, 0, 0] tilts cube 90° around X (long axis vertical)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cube", prim_type="Cube", position=[-0.9, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05, rotation_euler=[90.0, 0.0, 0.0])
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Cube", schema_name=api)

# sleepThreshold + rubber
bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=["/World/Cube"],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
apply_physics_material(prim_path="/World/Cube", material_name="rubber")

# Flip-wall: thin static collider on the belt. Cube hits, tips forward.
create_prim(prim_path="/World/FlipWall", prim_type="Cube", position=[0.4, 0.4, 0.83], scale=[0.01, 0.15, 0.025])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/FlipWall", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Landing-zone marker: small static prim past the flip-wall, INSIDE the conveyor's xy bbox
# so verify_pickplace_pipeline's cube_source_bridged sees the conveyor as the bridge.
# Cube settles here after tipping; sensor at this xy triggers controller pick.
create_prim(prim_path="/World/LandingZone", prim_type="Cube", position=[0.6, 0.4, 0.81], scale=[0.05, 0.05, 0.001])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/LandingZone", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Bin behind robot (cube ends upright in bin)
create_bin(prim_path="/World/Bin", position=[1.0, -0.4, 0.75], size=[0.3, 0.3, 0.15])

# Pick sensor centered on landing zone
add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/PickSensor", position=[0.6, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

# cuRobo controller — picks the now-upright cube from landing zone
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/Conveyor",
    source_paths=["/World/Cube"],
    destination_path="/World/Bin",
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/Conveyor", "/World/FlipWall", "/World/LandingZone", "/World/Bin"],
)
extension_notes: CP-05 adds a passive flip-station (FlipWall + LandingZone) before the pick zone. Cube starts with rotateXYZ=[90,0,0] (on side) and is expected to flip upright after hitting the wall. Function gate uses require_upright=True so test-pass requires cube_up_vector · world_up >= 0.95 at sim end. Hardest of the 4 canonicals because passive physics flips are sensitive to friction + collision geometry.
failure_modes: Cube doesn't reach the flip-wall at all — belt velocity too low, or cube rotated 90° around Z instead of X (not actually on side). Verify cube's rotateXYZ is [90, 0, 0] (X-axis tilt).
Cube hits flip-wall but bounces back instead of tipping — wall too tall. Reduce wall height (scale z) to ~0.025 (matches cube edge at z=0.83).

Cube tips forward past wall but doesn't land upright — wall too short OR cube's contact-friction with belt vs wall is unbalanced. Rubber on cube + default friction on wall should give it forward+down momentum.
Cube tips correctly but rolls past landing zone — landing zone too short OR cube has too much momentum. Increase landing zone scale[0] OR add a stop-wall behind landing zone.
Robot picks before cube settles — sensor triggers on tipping cube. Move sensor to centered on landing zone (currently [0.5, 0.4, 0.835]).

Robot picks tilted cube and drops tilted in bin — function gate (require_upright) catches this. Robot's natural pick orientation is palm-down; if cube is upright by then, pick + place preserves orientation.
verified_status: build-spec-2026-05-08; physics-tuning-required (passive flips are geometry-sensitive); visual delivery + orientation test pending user
